manualAssumptions.label: Non-smoker
manualAssumptions.description: Manual preference. StatCan does not publish this inside the same dating-friendly cross-tabs used here.

manualAssumptions.label: Wants children
manualAssumptions.description: Optional assumption slider for future-family alignment.

manualAssumptions.label: Politically compatible
manualAssumptions.description: Useful, subjective, and very much not in the Census.

population.title: Canada's population estimates, fourth quarter 2025
population.note: Latest quarterly population estimate used as the live starting population.

marital.title: Marital status, age group and gender: Canada, provinces and territories, census divisions, census subdivisions and dissemination areas
marital.note: Population aged 15 years and over, 100% data. Used for age, sex/gender, and relationship-status proxy steps.

education.title: Highest certificate, diploma or degree by age group and gender: Canada, provinces and territories
education.note: Population aged 15 years and over in private households. Education shares come from 25% sample census data.

income.title: Total income groups by age and gender: Canada, provinces and territories, census metropolitan areas and census agglomerations with parts
income.note: Income uses the Census income reference year 2020, which is older than the live 2026 population estimate.

ethnicity.title: Visible minority by gender and age: Canada, provinces and territories
ethnicity.note: Population-group filter uses official 2021 Census visible minority categories for persons in private households. Multi-select combines selected categories using union logic.

drinking.title: Risk level from alcohol consumption of Canadians aged 18 and older, by gender, age group and province, 2023
drinking.note: Drinking-habits shares use self-reported alcohol-use risk categories from the 2023 Canadian Community Health Survey. They are survey-based estimates, not census counts, and may underreport true consumption.

height.title: Measured standing height, by age and sex, household population, Canada, 2009 to 2011
height.note: Height uses measured standing-height percentiles from an official Canadian health survey. The calculator estimates surviving shares by interpolating across published percentiles.

language.title: Knowledge of official languages by age: Canada, provinces and territories
language.note: Language filter uses official-language knowledge categories rather than app usage or preference.

manual.title: User-entered manual assumption
manual.note: Not census-backed. This is a deliberate assumption slider applied after the statistical filters.
